title: FLEXIBLE SERVICES - 211info
text: OREGON STATE DIVISION OF MEDICAL ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS FLEXIBLE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Flexible services or "flex funds," are non-covered services offered in addition to the covered benefits of the Oregon Health Plan (OHP) through Coordinated Care Organizations (CCOs) to improve care delivery and overall member health and well-being. Eligibility for services is determined by the CCO. Services can include but are not limited to: -Housing support (rent assistance, rental deposit assistance [requires move-in date], utility bills, housing programs for women and children) -Temporary motel stays (For individuals being discharged from the hospital; experiencing an extreme weather event; has a set move-in date for a new home and needs a temporary place to stay before then; or actively fleeing a domestic violence situation.) -Electronics such as cellphones, computers, and other communication devices -Specific educational materials and/or classes -Family and home resources (ex. items for child health and safety, parenting programs, household appliances, climate devices) -Gym memberships and exercise classes/equipment -Health and substance use prevention support All flexible services are determined on a case-by-case basis by the CCO. HRS are NOT intended to address emergency situations. CCOs are not required to offer flexible services. As flexible services are not a covered benefit, a denial cannot be appealed, but an OHP member can submit a complaint. 500 Summer Street NE E-49 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: -Must be a current member of the CCO -Member must be unable to receive requested service by any other benefit or means -Item or service requested must help with medical need or life situation -All documentation requested for service must be provided Health-related services are limited, not immediate, not guaranteed or continuously available, and individuals may not qualify. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 945-5772 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (503) 945-5772 Administration (800) 273-0557 Toll-free Main phone (503) 945-5772 Toll-free (800) 273-0557 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: CCO members: Call the CCO or visit website for more information Open card members: Call or email for information Clients not in a CCO: Call for information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: Varies by service LANGUAGES: Arabic, Chinese, English, Spanish, Russian, Vietnamese, interpretation or signing by arrangement AREA SERVED: State of Oregon SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Services: Air Conditioners * Medicaid Recipients Cooking Classes * Medicaid Recipients Electric Service Payment Assistance * Medicaid Recipients Fitness Equipment and Accessories * Medicaid Recipients Furniture * Medicaid Recipients Gas Service Payment Assistance * Medicaid Recipients Health Related Temporary Housing * Medicaid Recipients Homeless Motel Vouchers * Medicaid Recipients Internet Service Payment Assistance * Medicaid Recipients Mobile Devices * Medicaid Recipients Rent Payment Assistance * Medicaid Recipients Rental Deposit Assistance * Medicaid Recipients Sewer Service Payment Assistance * Medicaid Recipients Substance Use Disorder Referrals * Medicaid Recipients Telephone Service Payment Assistance * Medicaid Recipients Therapeutic Exercise * Medicaid Recipients Trash/Recycling Service Payment Assistance * Medicaid Recipients Water Service Payment Assistance * Medicaid Recipients Other Services Offered At This Location: CONSUMER ASSISTANCE AND PROTECTION - OHP CLIENT SERVICES UNIT PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - OREGON HEALTH PLAN SPECIALTY MEDICINE - OREGON VETERAN DENTAL PROGRAM If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. OREGON STATE DIVISION OF MEDICAL ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS
